article_title: sbt 2.0 Enters Its Final Stretch as Twelve Release Candidates Bring Scala's Premier Build Tool to the Edge of a Stable Release
summary: Excellent article overall. One misattributed claim: '20 percent startup speed improvement' is attributed via inline link to the scala-sbt.org change summary, but that source only says 'improved startup performance' without the specific percentage.

content_preview.title: sbt 2.0 Enters Its Final Stretch as Twelve Release Candidates Bring Scala's Premier Build Tool to the Edge of a Stable Release
content_preview.summary: After years of development, sbt 2.0 has reached RC12 with 60+ ecosystem plugins ported, targeting a stable release within weeks to months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Scala community's de facto build tool is approaching a landmark milestone. As of mid-April 2026, sbt 2.0.0-RC12 is live, [the twelfth release candidate](https://github.com/sbt/sbt/releases) in a multi-year journey toward a stable major release. In a post titled ["Last mile towards sbt 2"](https://www.scala-lang.org/blog/2026/04/14/last-mile-towards-sbt2.html) published April 14 on the official Scala language blog, lead maintainer Eugene Yokota signaled that the final release is ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive, technically accurate article with excellent depth. Strong coverage of the plugin ecosystem, the sbt2-compat library, and the RC cadence. The Analysis section is well-reasoned.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fetched all 5 sources. Scala blog (April 14) confirms Eugene Yokota's 'weeks to a few months' quote, 60+ plugins, RC12 date of April 13, Scala Native 0.5.11, sbt-assembly 2.3.1, Play Framework milestone, Metals support, 2.0.x stabilization branch. sbt2-compat blog confirms Sovereign Tech Fund funding, HashedVirtualFileRef, VirtualFile, VirtualFileRef types. scala-sbt.org change summary confirms Scala 3 metabuild, incremental test (testFull), Bazel-compatible caching, project matrix, Maven BOM (.pomOnly()), native client startup improvement but NOT the specific 20% figure. JetBrains blog confirms JDK 17 requirement and warning display for sbt 2.x. GitHub releases page confirms RC10/RC11/RC12 dates and specific bug fixes.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One misattribution found: the inline link '[20 percent startup speed improvement](https://www.scala-sbt.org/2.x/docs/en/changes/sbt-2.0-change-summary.html)' attributes a specific percentage to the sbt change summary, but that document only confirms 'improved startup performance' without a numeric figure. No other source in the submission confirms the 20% figure. The specific percentage should either be sourced to a document that contains it, or the claim should be rephrased as 'measurable startup improvement' or similar.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Good balance of factual reporting and contextual analysis.
editor_notes.originality: No prior sbt 2.0 coverage in the archive.
concerns: The '20 percent startup speed improvement' in the sbtn native client bullet is attributed via inline link to scala-sbt.org/2.x/docs/en/changes/sbt-2.0-change-summary.html, but that source only says 'improved startup performance' without a specific percentage. Either find the source that contains this figure and link to it, or replace '20 percent' with a non-specific qualifier like 'measurable' or 'notable'.
recommendations: Find and link to a source that contains the 20% startup improvement figure, or rephrase to remove the specific percentage
editor_notes.overall_assessment: One of the better-researched technical pieces in this batch. The single misattribution is easily fixed. Strong candidate for publication once corrected.
